documentation.value: Restricts the following declarations to hosts matching the given patterns. Use `*` for wildcard matching.
A hostname pattern. `*` matches any string, `?` matches any single character, `!` negates the pattern.
Specifies the real hostname to log into. Numeric IP addresses are also permitted. This allows aliases in the Host directive.
The actual hostname or IP address to connect to
Specifies the user to log in as on the remote machine.
The remote username for SSH login
Specifies the port number to connect to on the remote host.
The port number (default is 22)
Specifies a file from which the user's identity key is read for public key authentication.
Path to the private key file, e.g. `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`
Specifies one or more jump proxies to connect through before reaching the target host.
A jump host in the format `[user@]host[:port]`, or comma-separated list for chained jumps
Specifies whether the authentication agent connection will be forwarded to the remote machine.
`yes` to forward the agent, `no` to disable forwarding (default: no)
Specifies that a TCP port on the local machine be forwarded over the secure channel to a specified host and port from the remote machine.
Local address and port to listen on
Remote host and port to forward to
Specifies that a TCP port on the remote machine be forwarded over the secure channel to a specified host and port from the local machine.
Remote address and port to listen on
Local host and port to forward to
Controls whether SSH automatically adds host keys to the known_hosts file and whether it refuses connections to hosts with changed keys.
`yes` never auto-add, `no` auto-add, `ask` prompt user, `accept-new` auto-add new but reject changed
